insight: Bishop Discipline Wing ships as A&A #2295 Tier 3 operational validation. Five Augurs (Librarian/Toolsmith/Pricing/Securities-Language/Closeout) composed into one Squadron, one Wing, one Consensus Layer. 12/12 verification checks pass K514. Wing engine evaluates all Augurs in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor; ConsensusLayer arbitrates per priority (critical-override > advisory). Append-only telemetry to wing_telemetry.jsonl. Helm PWA WingDashboard surfaces firing trends. Hook generalized from single-Augur to Wing-engine delegate — backward compat preserved.

Wing Augur trigger logic: when both file_path_patterns AND text_patterns specified, use AND semantics (path = scope filter, text = actual trigger). OR logic causes false positives: any .md file path match alone would fire Augur-Securities-Language even on benign content. AND semantics discovered via C.3/C.6/C.8/C.9 failures in Phase C; fixed in engine.py _evaluate_single_augur. Critical pattern for all future Augur configs.

ConsensusLayer arbitration: CRITICAL class Augurs fire -> BLOCK (critical-override, wins over all advisory signals). ADVISORY class Augurs fire -> WARN (any advisory signal warns). No Augur fires -> ALLOW. Priority hierarchy: block > warn > enrich > allow. Multi-Augur case (C.7): critical Augur-Librarian blocks even when advisory Augur-Toolsmith also signals. Advisory-only case (C.8): warn but not block. Configurable via bishop_wing_augurs.json consensus_rules section.

Single-rule Claude Code hook -> multi-rule Wing: keep hook at same path (settings.json unchanged), hook becomes thin stdin->engine->exit dispatcher, original rule becomes one Augur config in the engine. Fail-safe: engine import error -> sys.exit(0) (never block on hook bugs). Workspace path added to sys.path in hook for discipline_wing import. Pattern reusable for any future hook-to-Wing generalization.

#2295 Wing primitive sovereignty: Augur authors retain authority over their own rules; Wing composes without modifying individual Augur logic. Telemetry stays local to Bishop (wing_telemetry.jsonl in ~/.claude/state/). Cross-Wing federation (NAF/MAJCOM) is K519 territory. This session validates Tier 3; Tiers 4-8+ remain ahead. The Wing is the atomic unit of cross-Squadron coordination.

wing_telemetry.jsonl (append-only) records every PreToolUse evaluation: tool, file_path, per-Augur trace (triggered/signal/reason/elapsed_ms), triggered_augurs[], consensus_decision, consensus_reason, elapsed_ms. Bishop can query 'last 24h Wing telemetry' to surface drift trends. Helm PWA WingDashboard renders this live. Telemetry failure is silently swallowed (never breaks the hook). C.10 verifies telemetry grows and last line is valid JSON with augur_results + consensus_decision.

Root .gitignore line 232: *.py ignores ALL Python files. discipline_wing/*.py required explicit carve-outs added at .gitignore line 500 block: !discipline_wing/, !discipline_wing/__init__.py, !discipline_wing/engine.py, !discipline_wing/consensus.py, !discipline_wing/tests.py, !discipline_wing/README.md. Confirmed with git check-ignore -v (! prefix = carve-out active). TS-036 class: always run git check-ignore before first commit of new .py files in workspace.

Python test output with Unicode symbols (checkmark U+2713, X U+2717) fails on Windows PowerShell with UnicodeEncodeError in cp1252 codec. Fix: use ASCII equivalents (OK/XX instead of checkmarks) OR set $env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8' before running. Prefer ASCII-only test output for cross-platform portability. Applies to any Python script printing to PowerShell terminal.

Augur-Securities-Language uses file_path_patterns (['.md$','.ts$','.tsx$',...]) as scope + text_patterns (equity stake, shareholder, dividend, ROI, invest*, etc.) as trigger. AND semantics required: only fire when BOTH path is relevant AND text contains forbidden term. Exclusion paths (discipline_wing/, A&A formal files, counsel letters) bypass this Augur to avoid false positives in patent/legal documentation context where these terms appear legitimately.

A&A #2295 Tier 3 (Wing) reduction-to-practice anchor: Bishop Wing MVP committed K514 [2026-04-26], tag v-bishop-wing-mvp-K514. Files: discipline_wing/engine.py (Wing engine), discipline_wing/consensus.py (Consensus Layer), ~/.claude/state/wing_augurs/*.json (5 Augur configs), ~/.claude/hooks/bishop_librarian_gate.py (generalized hook), librarian-mcp-helm-pwa WingDashboard.tsx. Empirical proof: 12/12 verification checks pass; Wing is LIVE and enforcement-active.

WingDashboard.tsx added to Helm PWA (librarian-mcp-helm-pwa/src/renderer/src/components/). Integrated into App.tsx as 'wing' view with nav item 'Wing'. Fetches telemetry from daemon /wing/telemetry endpoint (AbortSignal.timeout 2s); falls back to localStorage cache; falls back to mock records for browser-mode. Shows: 5 Augur cards (class badge, fire count), stats row (total/blocked/warned/allowed), per-record list with decision badge + expandable per-Augur trace. Read-only v1; rule editing is K513/DisciplineRulesPanel territory.

C.12 regression test: prior B126 Augur-Librarian-only behavior preserved. With fresh Librarian state (~/.claude/state/bishop_last_librarian_consult.ts = current epoch), Augur-Librarian does NOT fire even on gated path -> decision=allow or augur_librarian not in triggered list. This confirms Wing generalization didn't change the single-Augur guard behavior. The Wing adds 4 siblings without affecting the original Librarian gate semantics.

Bishop Discipline Wing fail-safe design: (1) engine errors -> allow (sys.exit(0)); (2) missing Augur config -> skip that Augur, don't break the Wing; (3) Augur evaluation timeout (3s per future) -> graceful AugurResult with error reason; (4) telemetry write failure -> silently swallowed; (5) Wing config missing -> use safe defaults (empty augur list). The hook must NEVER block legitimate work due to its own bugs. Safety first, enforcement second.

Wing engine uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(len(augur_configs), 5)) for parallel Augur evaluation. Per-Augur timeout: future.result(timeout=3) — 3 seconds per Augur before graceful fallback. All Augurs evaluate in parallel; ConsensusLayer receives all results. Total wing evaluation time in tests: ~15-20ms for 5 Augurs. Pattern reusable for any N-rule parallel evaluation system.

Augur required_consult type semantics: (1) 'state_file': reads timestamp file, checks freshness_seconds -- returns True (satisfied, don't fire) if fresh; (2) 'text_contains': pattern check on content being written -- returns True if pattern found (safety condition met); (3) 'none': always returns False (Augur fires based on trigger alone, no consult escape hatch). Securities-Language uses 'none' because there's no safe consultation that makes forbidden terms acceptable -- the trigger alone is the block condition.

K514 Phase C test isolation issue: tests that set stale Librarian state (test_c2, test_c7) must be followed by tests that reset to fresh state. Without this, subsequent tests pick up stale state and get false positive blocks from Augur-Librarian. _set_librarian_ts(fresh=True) called at start of each advisory/allow test to isolate Librarian state from the test. Pattern: each test must set up its own preconditions explicitly -- don't rely on test order.
